title: 10 best herbal capsules for nasal congestion

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with nasal congestion because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages. The herbal remedy also contains compounds that have a decongestant effect, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the sinuses. Additionally, purple coneflower's antibacterial properties can help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to congestion. Overall, these properties make purple coneflower capsules a natural and effective solution for alleviating nasal congestion.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine: Purple coneflower contains compounds that have anti-allergic effects, helping to reduce histamine release and alleviate symptoms of nasal congestion, sneezing, and runny nose.

Anti-inflammatory: The herb's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and phenolic acids, possess anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby easing congestion.

Expectorant: Purple coneflower has a natural expectorant effect, helping to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, including the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and alleviate congestion in the nasal passages.
Buds: The buds of the purple coneflower contain flavonoids, which have natural antihistamine properties that help to relieve nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea roots and dry them in a warm place for at least 7 days or air-dry for 2 weeks.
Crush dried echinacea purpurea roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 20g of powdered echinacea purpurea with 100g of potato starch in a bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal blend and seal them immediately to preserve potency.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze for longer preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: King of bitters capsules helps with nasal congestion because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work synergistically to loosen and clear out mucus from the nasal passages. The natural ingredients, such as goldenseal, ginger, and slippery elm, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the nasal tissues and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, the capsules stimulate the mucociliary clearance mechanism, helping to remove excess mucus and debris, providing fast and effective relief from congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptus oil: Helps to decongest and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Peppermint oil: Provides a cooling sensation that can help to thin out mucus, reducing its thickness and making it easier to expel.
Ginger root: Has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the King of Bitters plant are used to make medicinal capsules for nasal congestion due to their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems of the plant, are used in nasal congestion treatments as they possess anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and ease congestion.
Barks: The barks of the King of Bitters plant contain flavonoids and terpenes that work together to thin mucus and make it easier to expel, providing relief from nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200 grams of andrographis paniculata leaves and flowers that are free of pesticides and other contaminants.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm, dry place for at least 7 days or use a food dehydrator on low heat.
Grind 100 grams of dried andrographis paniculata into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered andrographis with 500 ml of water to form a decoction, simmering it for 20 minutes then straining it through cheesecloth.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 250 milligrams of the cooled decoction mixture and seal immediately for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Umckaloabo capsules helps with nasal congestion because they contain a unique blend of natural herbs that work together to soothe and decongest the nasal passages. The mucilages present in these capsules form a protective barrier that helps to reduce inflammation and irritation, making it easier for the body to expel excess mucus and fluids. As a result, Umckaloabo capsules can provide fast and effective relief from sinus pressure, stuffiness, and congestion, allowing for easier breathing and improved overall health.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Umckaloabo capsules contain mucilages, which are gel-like substances that help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by congestion.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in Umckaloabo capsules help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate nasal congestion and sinusitis, promoting overall respiratory health.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory compounds found in Umckaloabo capsules, such as saponins and phenolic acids, help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, alleviating congestion and making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in saponins, which help to break down mucus and reduce nasal congestion.
Barks: The bark is high in tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a congested nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pelargonium sidoides leaves and stems in the morning when dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the harvested material using a food dehydrator or sun drying method for 6-8 hours.
Grind dried pelargonium sidoides into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250mg of ground pelargonium sidoides with 50mg of tapioca starch and 100mg of magnesium stearate in a bowl.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the mixed powder, using 500mg per capsule and sealing them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with nasal congestion because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When taken internally, glycyrrhizin is absorbed into the bloodstream and reaches the nasal passages, where it reduces swelling and constriction of blood vessels caused by allergies or colds. This helps to ease breathing and relieve sinus pressure, making it easier to breathe and sleep. The capsules also help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, and soothe irritated mucous membranes, providing fast and effective relief from nasal congestion.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing and relief from congestion.
Licoricidin: Has natural anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to soothe and calm the nasal tissues, reducing congestion and discomfort.
Glycyrrhetic acid: Acts as a expectorant, helping to loosen and clear out mucus and other debris from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain compounds like glycyrrhizin, which helps to reduce inflammation and ease congestion in the nasal passages.
Barks: Licorice barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to relax the nasal mucosa and reduce swelling, making it an effective ingredient for relieving nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Dry 250g of glycyrrhiza glabra roots for 2-3 hours in a low-temperature oven at 60°c.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 20g of the powdered licorice root with 100g of tapioca starch and 50g of gelatin to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty capsules with approximately 250-500mg of the herbal mixture and seal immediately after filling.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container for up to 6 months, keeping them away from direct sunlight and moisture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with nasal congestion because they contain a rich source of mucilages, which are natural thickening agents that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the nose. These mucilages help to break down excess mucus and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe and relieving congested sinuses. Additionally, the capsules may also contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and promote healthy drainage of the nasal passages, further alleviating congestion.
remedy_properties: Echinacea: Known for its anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, echinacea may help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and promote mucus clearance.

Eucalyptus: The essential oils extracted from eucalyptus leaves have decongestant and expectorant properties, which can help relieve nasal congestion and coughs by thinning mucus and making it easier to expel.
Marshmallow root: This herb has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that may help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and provide temporary relief from nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of saponins, which help to break down mucus and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm irritated nasal tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves and wash them thoroughly in cold water to remove impurities.
Dry the washed leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 12 hours or until crispy.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered ginkgo biloba with 400mg of gelatin capsules to create the herbal supplement.
Fill the gelatin capsules by spooning the ginkgo biloba mixture into the caps, then sealing them tightly by hand.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsules helps with nasal congestion because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds, including eucalyptol and cineole. These natural oils work together to ease congestion by thinning mucus, reducing inflammation, and opening up airways, making it easier to breathe. As the herbs are absorbed into the bloodstream, they can reach the nasal passages and sinuses, providing targeted relief from stuffiness, sinus pressure, and postnasal drip, helping to clear out the nose and promote a sense of ease and comfort.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's expectorant properties help loosen and thin mucus in the nasal passages, making it easier to expel, reducing congestion.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Tasmanian Blue Gum capsules reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal tissues, alleviating sinus pressure and congestion.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents in Tasmanian Blue Gum capsules help combat infections that can cause nasal congestion, such as colds, flu, and allergies, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi.
remedy_parts: Barks: The bark of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contains bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and ease congestion in the nasal passages, making it an effective ingredient for nasal congestion relief.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the plant has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm and soothe the nasal tissues, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tasmanian blue gum leaves eucalyptus globulus in quantity for processing.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven at 60°c for two hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using an electric coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part of the powdered tasmanian blue gum with four parts of organic gelatin filling capsule shells.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the mixture and seal tightly for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with nasal congestion because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains a compound called taraxasterol, which has been shown to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing relief from congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, dandelion's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe and promoting overall respiratory health. By supporting healthy lung function, dandelion capsules can help alleviate symptoms of chronic nasal congestion and promote a clearer airway.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing relief from congestion.
Expectorant: The herbal extract has expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieve congestion.

Antihistamine-like effects: Dandelion capsules have been shown to have antihistamine-like effects, which can help to reduce the production of mucus and relieve nasal congestion caused by allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like taraxasterol and taraxeryl acetate, which help reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
Fruits: Containing antioxidants and flavonoids, which work together to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, alleviating congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh or dried dandelion roots in the morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested dandelion roots for at least four hours using shade and air circulation.
Grind the dried dandelion roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder within three minutes.
Mix one part of the ground dandelion root powder with two parts of gelatin capsule powder, then fill 500-milligram capsules within eight minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months, shaking before use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with nasal congestion because of its natural decongestant properties. The menthol in peppermint relaxes the muscles in the nose and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness in the nasal passages, further opening up airways. As a result, peppermint capsules can provide fast and effective relief from stuffy noses and sinus pressure, making it an excellent natural remedy for alleviating respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Decongestant Properties: Peppermint capsules help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing and relief from congestion.
Mucolytic Properties: The menthol in peppermint capsules helps to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing the thickness and stickiness of nasal secretions.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Peppermint's active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have anti-inflammatory effects that help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain in the nasal passages and sinuses.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other bioactive compounds, peppermint roots are used to create decongesting and anti-inflammatory capsules that help alleviate nasal congestion.
Rhyzomes: Peppermint rhyzomes contain a high concentration of menthone, which provides fast relief from nasal congestion by reducing swelling and opening up airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves from a healthy plant.
Dry the peppermint leaves in a warm, dry place.
Crush the dried peppermint leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered peppermint with 200-250 ml of vodka or 95% ethanol as a solvent.
Fill empty capsules with the peppermint extract and store them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with nasal congestion because of their natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb sage contains compounds like thujone and camphor, which work to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, sage's antimicrobial properties help combat infections that can contribute to congestion, while its expectorant properties aid in loosening and eliminating mucus, providing relief from stuffy noses and sinuses. This natural approach can provide effective relief from nasal congestion without resorting to harsh chemical medications.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage's antimicrobial compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause nasal congestion, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Expectorant properties: Sage's saponins and flavonoids increase mucus production, making it easier to expel excess mucus and thin out thickened mucous, relieving nasal congestion.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's polyphenolic compounds, such as carnosic acid, reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, alleviating swelling and constriction that can worsen nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and soothe nasal passages.
Leaves: Sage leaves contain essential oils that act as a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and opening airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry fresh sage leaves in small batches to preserve their potency and aroma.
Chop the dried sage leaves into smaller pieces to increase surface area for infusion.
Combine one part chopped dried sage with three parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Infuse the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes at low heat and gentle stirring.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and fill empty capsules with approximately 500mg of herbal extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with nasal congestion because they contain compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses. The ginger extract also has expectorant properties, meaning it helps to loosen and clear out mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, ginger's natural warming properties can help to increase blood flow to the nasal passages, further reducing congestion and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, relieving congestion.
Expectorant properties: Herbal ginger capsules contain expectorants like camphene, which help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Antioxidant activity: Ginger's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate nasal congestion by reducing oxidative stress and promoting a healthy respiratory system.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, of the ginger plant contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to relieve nasal congestion.
Roots: Ginger roots have been traditionally used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues such as nasal congestion, and their active compounds are often extracted for use in capsules.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes of about 2-3 inches long for maximum potency.
Clean the harvested rhizomes thoroughly with distilled water and dry them completely with paper towels.
Peel the dried rhizomes using a vegetable peeler or sharp knife to remove the skin.
Grind the peeled rhizome into fine powder using a spice grinder or coffee grinder for about 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 200-250mg of powdered ginger and seal tightly for storage and use as needed within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cloves capsules helps with nasal congestion because they contain eugenol, a natural compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. When taken orally in capsule form, eugenol is absorbed into the bloodstream and travels to the nasal passages, where it helps to reduce swelling and relieve congestion caused by colds, allergies, or sinusitis. The warm, comforting sensation of cloves also promotes easy breathing and sinus drainage, providing fast and effective relief from nasal congestion.
remedy_properties: Eugenol: A key compound found in cloves, eugenol has natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing relief from congestion.

Antimicrobial properties: Clove capsules have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various bacteria and viruses that can cause respiratory infections, helping to combat infection and reduce sinus pressure.
Expectorant properties: The active compounds in clove capsules help to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and providing relief from congestion.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in eugenol, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce nasal congestion.
Buds: Contain cineole, a volatile oil that effectively relieves nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10g of dried syzygium aromaticum leaves from a reputable source and store them in an airtight container.
Grind the dried syzygium aromaticum leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 500mg of the ground syzygium aromaticum powder with 1000mg of gelatin capsule filler in a small bowl.
Fill empty #00 size gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture and seal them tightly to prevent contamination.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with nasal congestion because of its natural decongestant properties. The capsules contain a concentrated extract of lavender, which is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation and ease congestion. As the capsules are absorbed into the body, they work to thin mucus secretions, allowing for easier breathing and relief from stuffy noses. Additionally, lavender's calming effects can also help to reduce stress and promote a good night's sleep, further alleviating symptoms of nasal congestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.

Decongestant properties: The volatile oils in lavender, particularly camphor, have a natural decongestant effect, which helps to shrink blood vessels and reduce excess mucus production, alleviating congestion.
Expectorant properties: Lavender's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, lavender roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages.

Seeds: Containing a high concentration of essential oils, including linalool and camphor, lavender seeds provide natural decongestant and expectorant properties that can help relieve nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh or dried lavender flowers and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Combine the lavender flowers with 200ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another bowl, discarding the solids after 10 minutes.
Add 500mg of glycerin to the liquid extract and mix well before transferring it to a clean dark glass bottle.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the lavender-glycerin mixture each and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense capsules helps with nasal congestion because they contain boswellic acids, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds work to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing and relief from congestion. Additionally, frankincense has been shown to help loosen and clear mucus, further alleviating symptoms of sinus pressure and stuffiness. By promoting healthy sinus function, frankincense capsules can provide effective natural relief for individuals struggling with nasal congestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Frankincense contains boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing relief from congestion and sinus pressure.
Mucolytic properties: The active compounds in frankincense capsules, such as incensole acetate, help break down mucus in the nasal passages, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Frankincense has been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that may contribute to nasal congestion and sinusitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the frankincense plant contain a high concentration of boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing nasal congestion.

Buds: Frankincense buds contain a higher concentration of resinous sap than other parts of the plant, which is used to make medicinal capsules that help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Measure dried boswellia serrata root powder accurately according to recipe requirements.
Combine measured boswellia serrata root powder with silica gel in a glass bowl.
Gradually add vegetable glycerin and distilled water to create a uniform paste consistency.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the prepared boswellia serrata mixture using a capsule filler machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container for at least 2 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with nasal congestion because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, particularly curcuminoids. These compounds work to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing and improved airflow. Additionally, turmeric's natural decongestant properties help to clear out excess mucus, making it an effective remedy for relieving sinus pressure and congestion. By taking herbal turmeric capsules regularly, individuals can experience relief from chronic nasal congestion and breathe easier throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Cyclooxygenase (COX) inhibition: Turmeric's active compound curcumin inhibits COX enzymes, which are responsible for producing pro-inflammatory chemicals that contribute to nasal congestion.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities: Curcumin's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nasal passages, while its anti-inflammatory effects alleviate swelling and congestion.

Mucus-thinning and decongestant actions: Turmeric contains bioactive compounds that have natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear thick mucus from the nasal passages, reducing congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant contain a high concentration of curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help relieve nasal congestion.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of turmeric are rich in bioactive compounds like curcuminoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function, making them effective for treating nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 250 milliliters of dried turmeric root powder.
Mix with 500 milliliters of vegetable oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for one hour to extract the bioactive compounds.
Strain the oil and discard the solids then add 250 milliliters of water.
Fill capsules with the liquid extract using a capsule machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with nasal congestion because they contain mucilages that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the nose, throat, and sinuses. These natural compounds thicken mucus, making it easier to expel, and reduce inflammation caused by allergies or colds. As a result, herbal marshmallow capsules can provide rapid relief from nasal congestion, allowing for clearer breathing and sinus drainage. This natural remedy is an effective alternative to synthetic decongestants and offers long-lasting benefits without the need for repeated dosing.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow contains a high amount of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and lubricates the respiratory tract, helping to relieve nasal congestion and discomfort.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in marshmallow capsules help to reduce inflammation and protect against oxidative stress, which can exacerbate nasal congestion and other respiratory issues.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a natural thickening agent that helps soothe and protect irritated nasal passages.
Rhizomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and ease sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis from a trusted supplier.
Grind the dried marshmallow root into a fine powder using an electric coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 250g of the powdered marshmallow root with 125ml of boiling distilled water to create a decoction for 10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a glass measuring cup and discard solids after 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the cooled decoction and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with nasal congestion because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that work together to soothe and clear out mucus from the nasal passages. The natural properties in elderberries, such as quercetin and anthocyanins, help to reduce swelling and ease sinus pressure, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, elder capsules have been traditionally used to support immune function, which can also contribute to their effectiveness in relieving nasal congestion.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory compounds: Elderberry extracts contain vasodilatory compounds like anthocyanins and flavonoids, which help to dilate blood vessels in the nasal passages, reducing swelling and congestion.
Antioxidant activity: Herbal elder capsules are rich in antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress that exacerbates nasal congestion.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Elderberry contains anti-inflammatory compounds like quercetin and isoquercitrin, which have been shown to reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with sinusitis and nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots are used to make capsules as they contain powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, alleviating congestion.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the elder plant is rich in antioxidants and has natural decongestant properties, making it effective for relieving nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers in mid-summer when they are fully bloomed and dry them completely to preserve their potency.
Measure out 30 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers and mix them with 10 grams of cornstarch in a clean glass bowl.
Grind the mixture into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, taking about 15 minutes to achieve a uniform texture.
Fill 100 empty capsules with 250 milligrams of the powdered sambucus nigra mixture each, sealing them tightly after filling.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or freeze them for longer preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with nasal congestion because they contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages. The capsules also contain antioxidants that combat oxidative stress and protect the mucous membranes from damage. Additionally, pot marigold has natural expectorant properties, loosening mucus and making it easier to expel, providing relief from congestion and sinus pressure. By promoting a healthy inflammatory response and clearing out excess mucus, pot marigold capsules can help alleviate nasal congestion and promote clearer breathing.
remedy_properties: Calendula Oil: Has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which helps to reduce swelling and infection in the nasal passages, relieving congestion.
Quercetin: A flavonoid antioxidant that acts as a natural decongestant, helping to reduce excess mucus production and alleviate sinus pressure.
Beta-Carotene: Converted to vitamin A in the body, it helps to stimulate the mucous membranes, promoting healthy drainage and reducing congestion in the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain calcium, magnesium, and potassium, which can help to soothe and calm the nasal mucosa, reducing congestion and promoting drainage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in early morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested flowers using a warm air circulation method for 24 hours.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 500mg of powdered calendula with 250mg of gelatin and capsule size 0 empty capsules.
Fill the filled capsules in an airtight container and store at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop capsules helps with nasal congestion because they contain the potent herb hyssop, which has natural decongestant properties. When taken as a supplement, hyssop helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing and sinus drainage. Additionally, hyssop's antimicrobial properties help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to congestion, promoting overall respiratory health. By targeting both the physical and microbial aspects of nasal congestion, hyssop capsules can provide effective relief from stuffy noses and sinuses.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Hyssop's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.

Antihistamine-like effects: Hyssop contains compounds that have antihistamine-like effects, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, alleviating symptoms of congestion and sinus pressure.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Hyssop's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness and swelling in the nose and sinuses, further relieving congestion and promoting easier breathing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, hyssop roots are used to create capsules that help reduce nasal congestion and inflammation.
Barks: The bark of hyssop contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, making it effective for relieving nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hyssopus officinalis leaves from a trusted source in sufficient quantity for the desired number of capsules.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 6 hours or air drying for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, aiming for a dosage of 250mg per capsule.
Mix the powdered hyssopus officinalis with a carrier agent such as silica or calcium carbonate in a ratio of 1:2, filling capsules to 500mg capacity.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature, allowing them to steep for at least 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with nasal congestion because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the nasal passages. The apigenin and luteolin flavonoids present in chamomile work together to reduce swelling and inflammation, allowing for easier breathing and relief from stuffiness. Additionally, chamomile's mucilages help to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel, further alleviating congestion symptoms. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective way to alleviate nasal congestion without harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile capsules reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, helping to alleviate congestion and sinus pressure.

Antihistamine-like effects: The flavonoids present in chamomile capsules have a similar effect to antihistamines, which helps to block histamine receptors and relieve symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and congestion.
Natural expectorant properties: Chamomile capsules help loosen and clear out mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce swelling and alleviate congestion in the nasal passages.
Flowers: Contain azulene, a natural antispasmodic that relaxes and calms the mucous membranes, providing relief from nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in quantities of 50 grams.
Grind the dried flowers into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix one part ground matricaria chamomilla flowers with two parts filler material such as silica gel for 5 minutes.
Fill empty #0 capsules with the herbal mixture and seal according to manufacturer's instructions within 30 seconds.
Store the finished herbal capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with nasal congestion because of its natural expectorant properties, which loosen and clear out mucus from the airways. The compounds present in fennel, such as anethole and trans-anethole, stimulate the production of mucous, making it easier to expel and breathe freely. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory effects help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for improved airflow and sinus drainage, providing instant relief from congestion and sinus pressure. This natural remedy is a safe and effective way to alleviate nasal congestion symptoms.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Fennel's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal tissues, which can contribute to congestion and sinus pressure.

Natural mucolytic property: Fennel's natural mucolytic properties help break down mucus into smaller particles that are easier to expel, making it an effective treatment for chronic sinusitis and nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain oil-rich compounds that help loosen mucus and provide quick relief from nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare seeds, ensure they are free of contaminants and stored properly.
Dry the seeds in a warm place or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 2-3 hours.
Grind the dried seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250mg of ground foeniculum vulgare with 500mg of gelatin and 100mg of silica in a bowl, stirring well to combine.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal immediately, store in an airtight container for up to 2 months.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for nasal congestion are a natural remedy that provides relief from stuffy noses and sinus pressure. These capsules contain potent herbs such as eucalyptus, peppermint, and ginger that help to thin mucus and open airways, alleviating congestion. For example, Echinacea and Goldenseal capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the nasal passages, while Thyme capsules have antimicrobial properties that combat infections. By taking these herbal capsules, individuals can breathe easier, sleep better, and enjoy improved overall health and quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat nasal congestion with herbal capsules is to combine Echinacea and Goldenseal. Echinacea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, while Goldenseal's antimicrobial effects combat infection-causing bacteria and viruses. Take 2-3 capsules of a standardized extract blend twice daily for optimal relief. Additionally, consider adding Marshmallow root to soothe and protect the mucous membranes. Consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nasal congestion that are treated with herbal capsules are sinusitis, bronchitis, and asthma. These respiratory issues often involve inflammation and constriction of airways, making breathing difficult. Herbal capsules containing ingredients such as ginger, turmeric, and eucalyptus help to reduce inflammation, open up air passages, and ease congestion.
